directors.name: Maya Alvarez
directors.bio: Maya Alvarez is a resident director known for contemporary reinterpretations of classic musicals. Since joining the company in 2014, she has directed multiple award-winning productions including several family-friendly musical revivals.

directors.name: James Okafor
directors.bio: James Okafor specializes in Shakespeare and poetic drama, with a focus on performances for student audiences. He regularly directs weekday matinee productions tailored for high school groups.

directors.name: Lena Kapoor
directors.bio: Lena Kapoor is an experimental theatre director whose work centers on new play development and intimate dramas. She collaborates closely with playwrights to bring new voices to the mainstage.

directors.name: Samuel Choi
directors.bio: Samuel Choi directs large-scale musical productions featuring diverse casts and innovative choreography. Between 2015 and 2019 he helmed a series of hit musicals that remain audience favorites.

directors.name: Claire Benson
directors.bio: Claire Benson focuses on youth and family theatre, creating productions that are accessible, interactive, and engaging for audiences ages 8 and up.

funds.name: Youth Education & Student Matinee Fund
funds.short_description: Supports youth education programs, student matinees, and in-school theatre workshops.
funds.long_description: The Youth Education & Student Matinee Fund underwrites subsidized tickets for school groups, transportation assistance for Title I schools, and teaching artist residencies that bring theatre into classrooms. Monthly gifts help sustain year-round education outreach and curriculum-aligned workshops.

funds.name: Annual Fund for General Operations
funds.short_description: Provides flexible support for productions, staffing, and day-to-day operations.
funds.long_description: Gifts to the Annual Fund sustain every aspect of the theatre’s work—from keeping the lights on and the stages running to supporting fair wages for artists and staff. Unrestricted support allows the company to respond quickly to emerging needs.

funds.name: New Work & Commissions Fund
funds.short_description: Invests in the development of new plays and musicals.
funds.long_description: The New Work & Commissions Fund supports playwright commissions, workshop productions, and staged readings. Your contribution helps nurture bold new voices and expand the canon of contemporary theatre.

funds.name: Accessibility & Inclusion Fund
funds.short_description: Supports ASL-interpreted, captioned, and sensory-friendly performances.
funds.long_description: Gifts to the Accessibility & Inclusion Fund make the theatre more welcoming for everyone by underwriting ASL interpreters, open captioning, audio description, and sensory-friendly adaptations of select performances.

funds.name: Community & Family Engagement Fund
funds.short_description: Expands free and low-cost programs for families and community partners.
funds.long_description: This fund supports neighborhood partnerships, pay-what-you-can performances, and family-friendly events that invite new audiences into the theatre.

membership_plans.name: 4-Show Adult Flex Pass
membership_plans.short_description: Flexible package for adults seeing at least four shows per season.
membership_plans.long_description: The 4-Show Adult Flex Pass is designed for theatregoers who want flexibility and value. Each member receives four ticket credits per season that can be used on any standard performance, with free ticket exchanges up to 24 hours before showtime.
benefits: 4 ticket credits per season for any standard performance
Free ticket exchanges up to 24 hours before showtime
Priority booking window before single tickets go on sale

membership_plans.name: Premier 8-Show Subscription
membership_plans.short_description: Best for avid theatregoers who attend nearly every production.
membership_plans.long_description: The Premier 8-Show Subscription includes one ticket to each mainstage production in the season, the best available seating, free exchanges, and exclusive invitations to backstage events.
benefits: Free ticket exchanges and no service fees
Invitations to dress rehearsals and talkbacks

membership_plans.name: Family Matinee Pack
membership_plans.short_description: Discounted tickets for families attending youth-friendly matinees.
membership_plans.long_description: The Family Matinee Pack is tailored for households with children. Receive bundled pricing on weekend and holiday matinees marked as family-friendly, plus invitations to backstage tours designed for younger audiences.
benefits: Access to discounted family-friendly matinee tickets
Early access to youth & family program registrations

membership_plans.name: Student Rush Membership
membership_plans.short_description: Low-cost membership for students with same-day rush access.
membership_plans.long_description: The Student Rush Membership provides access to deeply discounted same-day rush tickets for current students with valid ID, plus occasional free workshop opportunities.
benefits: Access to $15 student rush tickets, subject to availability
Invitations to student-only talkbacks and masterclasses
Periodic email alerts for complimentary student nights

membership_plans.name: Preview Performance Pass
membership_plans.short_description: For adventurous audiences who love seeing shows in preview.
membership_plans.long_description: The Preview Performance Pass offers discounted access to preview performances before official opening nights. Ideal for those who enjoy seeing work-in-progress and supporting new plays.
benefits: Ticket to five preview performances per season

venues.name: Main Stage Theatre
venues.accessibility_notes: Step-free access from street to all orchestra-level seating. Wheelchair and companion seats available in multiple rows. ASL-interpreted and captioned performances scheduled regularly.

venues.name: Black Box Studio
venues.accessibility_notes: Accessible entry via rear ramp. Limited wheelchair seating available; please contact the box office in advance to reserve.

venues.name: Family Playhouse
venues.accessibility_notes: Designed with families in mind, featuring stroller parking, family restrooms, and sensory-friendly modifications at select performances.

venues.name: Rehearsal Hall A
venues.accessibility_notes: Elevator access from lobby to third floor. Used primarily for auditions, workshops, and staged readings.

productions.title: The Enchanted Clocktower
productions.synopsis: A curious 12-year-old discovers a magical clocktower that can rewind moments in time, sending her and her friends on a tuneful adventure to fix mistakes and learn from the past.
productions.education_notes: Ideal for family outings and upper-elementary school groups. Study guide available with activities on decision-making and empathy.

productions.title: Spring Musical
productions.synopsis: An original pop-rock musical following a group of graduating seniors as they stage one last show together and face the leap into adulthood.
productions.education_notes: Content and themes suitable for high school theatre programs. Limited student rush and group tickets available; see Education section for details.

productions.title: Romeo and Juliet
productions.age_recommendation: Recommended for ages 14 and up due to thematic material.
productions.synopsis: A fast-paced, contemporary take on Shakespeares tragic romance set in a divided modern city, featuring a diverse ensemble cast and a live onstage band.
productions.education_notes: Weekday matinee performances in October are designed for high school groups, with pre-show digital resources and optional post-show talkbacks focusing on language, conflict, and social dynamics.

productions.title: The Glass Menagerie
productions.synopsis: Tennessee Williams lyrical memory play about family, fragility, and the weight of expectations, staged with a vivid, dreamlike design.
productions.education_notes: Select performances offer ASL interpretation, open captioning, and audio description. Study materials available for high school and college literature classes.

productions.title: City of Bridges
productions.synopsis: Four siblings reunite in their changing hometown to decide the fate of the family business as the city around them rapidly transforms.
productions.education_notes: Talkbacks available after select performances focusing on urban change, activism, and community.

productions.title: Laughing Matters
productions.synopsis: A fast, physical ensemble comedy about a small-town improv troupe whose offstage drama is even wilder than their onstage antics.
productions.education_notes: Recommended for teen improv clubs and theatre students. Group workshops may be booked through the Education department.

productions.title: Bright City Nights
productions.synopsis: An original jazz-infused musical about a pianist trying to keep his late fathers club alive in a rapidly changing neighborhood.
productions.education_notes: Featured in the 2015 new musical development series.

productions.title: Harbor Lights
productions.synopsis: Set in a coastal town, this sweeping musical follows three generations of lighthouse keepers balancing tradition, change, and the pull of the sea.
productions.education_notes: Select performances were designated as family matinees with audience talkbacks.

productions.title: Skyline Dreams
productions.synopsis: A contemporary musical about three friends sharing a cramped city apartment, chasing big dreams while navigating love, money, and art.
productions.education_notes: Included a post-show series on making a career in the arts.

productions.title: Rhythm of the Rain
productions.synopsis: A folk-pop musical tracing one familys relationship to a river over the course of a century of floods, droughts, and renewals.
productions.education_notes: Family study guide focused on environmental stewardship and oral history.

productions.title: Summer on Maple Street
productions.synopsis: A nostalgic musical about a block of neighbors who band together to save their beloved corner store over one extraordinary summer.
productions.education_notes: Popular with youth and family audiences; featured an intergenerational community chorus.

productions.title: Hamlet
productions.synopsis: A lean, psychological staging of Shakespeares tragedy set in a surveillance state, spotlighting themes of paranoia and resistance.
productions.education_notes: Extensive high school matinee program with in-class workshops and post-show discussions.

productions.title: Family Tree
productions.synopsis: Three generations gather for a birthday weekend that unearths long-buried secrets and reshapes a familys understanding of its past.
productions.education_notes: Offered community talkbacks on family caregiving and intergenerational communication.

productions.title: The Snowy Owl
productions.synopsis: A winter adventure about a shy child who befriends a talking snowy owl and discovers courage during the longest night of the year.
productions.education_notes: Created for elementary school field trips with a companion classroom activity packet.

productions.title: The Comedy of Errors
productions.synopsis: A colorful, music-filled production of Shakespeares madcap farce, featuring physical comedy and mistaken identities galore.
productions.education_notes: Very popular with school groups; included pre-show clowning workshops for students.

productions.title: Rising Tide
productions.synopsis: An urgent new musical about climate activists across three continents, connected by rising seas and shared resistance.
productions.education_notes: Paired with community panels on climate justice and activism.

productions.title: Galaxy Questors
productions.synopsis: A sci-fi adventure for young audiences about a crew of kids who travel the galaxy collecting stories to save their home planets imagination.
productions.education_notes: Family performances included pre-show lobby activities and sensory-friendly adaptations at select dates.

audition_opportunities.title: Spring Musical 2026 - Principal & Ensemble Auditions
audition_opportunities.description: Open call for principal and ensemble roles in the 2026 Spring Musical, an original pop-rock production. Seeking strong singer-actors and movers of all ethnicities and body types, ages 18+.
audition_opportunities.location: Rehearsal Hall A, 75 Vine Street, Riverton, CA 94109
audition_opportunities.notes: Please prepare a 32-bar cut of a contemporary musical theatre or pop/rock song and bring a headshot and resume. Accompanist provided. Callbacks will be scheduled separately.

audition_opportunities.title: Romeo and Juliet 2026 - Casting Call
audition_opportunities.description: Seeking a diverse ensemble for a contemporary adaptation of Shakespeares Romeo and Juliet. All roles are open; experience with classical text is helpful but not required.
audition_opportunities.location: Main Stage Theatre, 123 Center Street, Riverton, CA 94110
audition_opportunities.notes: Prepare one 60-90 second Shakespeare monologue and one contrasting contemporary monologue. Sides may be provided at the audition.

audition_opportunities.title: The Glass Menagerie 2026 - Auditions
audition_opportunities.description: Auditions for Tennessee Williams The Glass Menagerie. Seeking actors with strong facility for lyrical text and intimate, detailed performance.
audition_opportunities.location: Black Box Studio, 145 Center Street, Suite B, Riverton, CA 94110
audition_opportunities.notes: Please prepare one contemporary dramatic monologue, 2 minutes or less. Some auditioners may be asked to read sides from the play.

audition_opportunities.title: The Enchanted Clocktower - Youth & Adult Roles
audition_opportunities.description: Casting both youth (ages 1017) and adult performers for this family-friendly new musical. Strong singers with movement skills are encouraged to audition.
audition_opportunities.location: Family Playhouse, 200 Riverside Drive, Riverton, CA 94111
audition_opportunities.notes: Youth performers should prepare a 16-bar cut of a song and a short memorized story or poem. Adults should prepare a 32-bar cut of an uptempo musical theatre piece.

audition_opportunities.title: Galaxy Questors - Touring Company Auditions
audition_opportunities.description: Casting performers for potential remount and touring version of the 2024 youth production Galaxy Questors.
audition_opportunities.location: Rehearsal Hall A, 75 Vine Street, Riverton, CA 94109
audition_opportunities.notes: Auditions by appointment only. Please prepare a short comedic monologue and 16 bars of a song suitable for young audiences.

audition_dates.notes: Evening session for general auditions; limited walk-in slots may be available.
Primary evening session for scheduled audition slots between 6:00�PM and 8:00�PM.
Weekend audition day; daytime and limited evening slots available.
Callback and overflow evening appointments; some new audition slots may open as space permits.
Final audition day for roles not yet cast.
Initial auditions focusing on younger roles and understudies.
General auditions open to all roles; some callbacks may be scheduled same evening.
Weekend session emphasizing ensemble and movement work.
Callbacks for principal roles; by invitation only.
Initial audition appointments for all roles.
Callback session focusing on scene work in pairs.
Open call for youth and adult performers; movement combination taught on site.
Scheduled appointments for invited callbacks only.
Auditions for touring company  vocal focus.
Callbacks emphasizing character work and physical storytelling.

audition_slots.notes: Ideal for performers needing an early evening time.
Includes brief time for questions after audition.
Morning slot; check in 15 minutes early.
General audition for Romeo and Juliet.

performances.notes: Family matinee with optional post-show cast meet-and-greet.
Family-friendly Saturday matinee. Youth group discounts available with advance booking.
Relaxed performance with adjusted lighting and sound levels, ideal for younger or sensory-sensitive audience members.
Weekday school matinee with optional post-show talkback. Minimum group size of 15 students required for group booking.
Public evening performance. Open captioning available in reserved seating section.
ASL-interpreted performance with reserved sightline seating for Deaf and hard-of-hearing patrons. Ticket prices for accessible seating start at $30 or less.
Standard evening performance. Audio description headsets available by advance request.
Sunday matinee performance with post-show discussion led by a guest scholar on select dates.
Intimate black box staging with limited seating. Post-show community dialogue after this performance.
Opening weekend performance of the summer comedy run.
Holiday family matinee with pre-show craft activities in the lobby.
High school student matinee with an extended post-show Q&A session.
